section_name: Authority to Take Abandoned Motor Vehicle into Custody
text:
(a) A law enforcement agency may take into custody an abandoned motor vehicle, aircraft, watercraft, or outboard motor found on public or private property.

(b) A law enforcement agency may use agency personnel, equipment, and facilities or contract for other personnel, equipment, and facilities to remove, preserve, store, send notice regarding, and dispose of an abandoned motor vehicle, aircraft, watercraft, or outboard motor taken into custody by the agency under this subchapter.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 683. Abandoned Motor Vehicles
##### § 683.011 — Authority to Take Abandoned Motor Vehicle into Custody

(a) A law enforcement agency may take into custody an abandoned motor vehicle, aircraft, watercraft, or outboard motor found on public or private property.

(b) A law enforcement agency may use agency personnel, equipment, and facilities or contract for other personnel, equipment, and facilities to remove, preserve, store, send notice regarding, and dispose of an abandoned motor vehicle, aircraft, watercraft, or outboard motor taken into custody by the agency under this subchapter.

*Acts 2011, 82nd Leg., R.S., Ch. 720 (H.B.787), Sec. 4, eff. September 1, 2011.*

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: A law enforcement agency may take into custody an abandoned motor vehicle, aircraft, watercraft, or outboard motor found on public or private property.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: A law enforcement agency may use agency personnel, equipment, and facilities or contract for other personnel, equipment, and facilities to remove, preserve, store, send notice regarding, and dispose of an abandoned motor vehicle, aircraft, watercraft, or outboard motor taken into custody by the agency under this subchapter.

history.raw: Acts 2011, 82nd Leg., R.S., Ch. 720 (H.B.787), Sec. 4, eff. September 1, 2011.
